title: supplier_scorecard.py output
description: Requires --component or --supplier (never neither). Row identity in component_breakdown depends on filters.component: if set, each row is one supplier (key: supplier_name); if null (cross-component run for one fixed supplier), each row is one component (key: component_category) instead — supplier_name is omitted since it's already fixed by filters.supplier. criticality_breakdown is the same axis with component_criticality added as a second groupby key. Optionally narrow further with --startdate/--enddate (YYYY-MM-DD, inclusive on both ends) — order-date range filtering, usable on any run regardless of whether --component and/or --supplier is set. This subsumes the retired supplier_landed_cost operation's only unique capability; total_actual_cost_per_unit/avg_unit_price here are the same spend-weighted formulas it used to export as total_actual_cost_per_unit/weighted_avg_unit_price.

supplier_name.description: Present only when filters.component is set.

component_category.description: Present only when filters.component is null (cross-component supplier run).

pct_spend_contribution.description: 0-100. This row's % share of total spend within the filtered scope.

avg_unit_price.description: Spend-weighted: total_spend / total_qty, not a plain mean of unit_cost_usd. Same formula the retired supplier_landed_cost.py exported as weighted_avg_unit_price.

total_actual_cost_per_unit.description: (total_spend + rework_cost) / total_qty. Identical formula the retired supplier_landed_cost.py exported under the same field name.

md_rate_pct_supplier_error.description: 0-100. Rate of defects attributed to supplier fault.

component_breakdown.description: Primary breakdown — one row per supplier (filters.component set) or per component (filters.component null). Same data previously exported under the key "suppliers".

criticality_breakdown.description: Same axis as component_breakdown, with component_criticality added as a second groupby key.
